The size-policy helper MUST use the canonical Qt 6 THREE-CALL escape sequence to leave setFixedWidth(120): (1) setMinimumWidth(0) (2) setMaximumWidth(16777215) [QWIDGETSIZE_MAX] (3) setSizePolicy(QSizePolicy.Expanding, QSizePolicy.Fixed). To go BACK to Fixed-120, setFixedWidth(120) is sufficient. See RESEARCH §Pattern 3 / Pitfall 1 — load-bearing landmine.

decisions.decision: Plan 02's three-call setFixedWidth↔Expanding round-trip is the load-bearing landmine for Wave 1
decisions.rationale: Volume slider's setFixedWidth(120) pins min==max==120; setSizePolicy() alone does NOT release that. Only setMinimumWidth(0) + setMaximumWidth(16777215) + setSizePolicy(Expanding, Fixed) works. Tested by test_widen_back_restores_cluster_to_row_1.
